ddls:
CREATE TABLE "local_councils" (
  "council_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each local council */,
  "ons_code" TEXT /* ONS code assigned to the local council */,
  "council_name" TEXT /* Name of the local council */,
  "established_date" TEXT /* Date the local council was established */,
  "website_url" TEXT /* Official website URL of the local council */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the local council */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the local council */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the local council */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("council_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policies" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy */,
  "council_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the local council that the policy belongs to */,
  "policy_name" TEXT /* Name of the policy */,
  "policy_description" TEXT /* Description of the policy */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the policy was last updated */,
  "published" TEXT /* Date the policy was published */,
  "submitted" TEXT /* Date the policy was submitted */,
  "found_sound" TEXT /* Date the policy found sound */,
  "adopted" TEXT /* Date the policy was adopted */,
  "document_url" TEXT /* URL of the policy document */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policies_council_id FOREIGN KEY ("council_id") REFERENCES local_councils ("council_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policy_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy update */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the policy that was updated */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the update was made */,
  "update_description" TEXT /* Description or details of the update */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* User who made the update */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the policy after the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policy_updates_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., council member, admin, public) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the policy accessed (if applicable) */,
  "update_id" INTEGER /* ID of the update accessed (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES policies ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_update_id FOREIGN KEY ("update_id") REFERENCES policy_updates ("update_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytic entry */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the policy being analyzed */,
  "metric" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., views, updates) */,
  "value" INTEGER /* Value of the metric */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the metric was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the permission applies */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the policy for which the permission is set */,
  "permission_level" TEXT /* Level of permission (e.g., read-only, edit, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES policies ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
